Petition For Approval Of Compromise Of Claim Or Action Or Disposition Of Proceeds Of Judgment For Minor (Ms. Ivanka Nahornaya)
Off Calendar Apr-14-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on the Petition to Approve Compromise of the Claim or Action of the Minor, Ivanka Nahornaya.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 11:08 a.m.

Aleksandr A. Volkov, Esq. appeared with petitioner Volha Nahornaya, who was present as Guardian ad Litem for the minor, Ivanka Nahornaya, who was also present.
Petitioner was placed under oath and testified.
Mr. Volkov, along with Ms. Volha Nahornaya and Ms. Ivanka Nahornaya, appeared via ZOOM for this morning's hearing.
Order appointing Volha Nahornaya Guardian ad Litem for the minor was filed January 14, 2022.

On the basis of evidence received, and good cause appearing, the Petition is granted and the Compromise is approved as follows:

Settlement Amount - $15,000.00.
Attorney Fees - $2,976.60.
Costs - $117.00.

The balance of $11,906.40 shall be deposited into a blocked account with JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A., located at 630 E. Blithedale Avenue, Mill Valley, CA 94941. No withdrawals shall be made without a written Court Order or until the minor reaches the age of 18 years.

Matter is CONTINUED to June 23, 2022, at 9:00 a.m. for Progress Report on the Deposit of Money into a Blocked Account.

Counsel for petitioner shall prepare the proposed Orders Approving the Compromise and to Deposit Money into a Blocked Account, and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

Proceedings concluded at 11:19 a.m.

DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-26-2021 Continued To Oct-01-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANTS SVIATLANA NAHORNAYA AND VOLHA NAHORNAYA'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is continued to October 1, 2021. In sustaining the prior demurrer, the Court ordered the cross-complaint to attach an English translation of the contract per CRC 3.1110(g). All causes of action alleged in this complaint are based in part on the allegation that Mr. Yakimchuk moved-in without prior knowledge/consent of the owner and/or that the intent to move Mr. Yakimchuk in was concealed from the owner. This allegation is contradicted by the language of the contract, which cross-complainant seeks to enforce: "At the end of the summer, you informed me of your circumstances in the family by asking to let you for a couple of months a second room for your newly arrived sister and her husband. Taking into account your circumstances and after discussing your situation with the members of my family, we decided to satisfy your request and allowed temporary dwelling for your sister and her husband." Cross-Complainant now claims that there is some "translation discrepancy" and that the contract "does not unequivocally state that Cross-Complainant was informed that. Mr. Yakimchuk would be moving in at the time. It merely states that as of December 2018, Mr. Yakimchuk was residing at the Property." (Opposition 5:23-28). By no later than September 24, 2021 cross-complaint to file a translation of the contract without "translation discrepanc[ies]" or provide a declaration that the translation attached to the complaint is the proper, code complaint translation of the contract attached to the second amended cross-complaint as an exhibit.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
